concepts.title: Equality of complex numbers
concepts.conceptBody:
Equality 

i.e., ${{z}_{1}}={{z}_{2}}$

this is true, only when $\text{Re}\left( {{\text{z}}_{1}} \right)=\text{Re}\left( {{\text{z}}_{2}} \right)\And $

$\text{Im}\left( {{z}_{1}} \right)=\text{Im}\left( {{z}_{2}} \right)$

Note: There is no order relation between complex numbers i.e., $\left( {{a}_{1}}+i{{b}_{1}} \right)>\left( {{a}_{2}}+i{{b}_{2}} \right)$ is not a valid inequality.

concepts.title: Important formulae for reciprocal of complex number
concepts.conceptBody:
Reciprocal/Multiplicative Inverse 

for complex $z=a+ib$

$\frac{1}{z}=\frac{1}{a+ib}$

& we know this is equal to $\frac{a-ib}{{{a}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}}$

here $a-ib=\overset{{}}{\mathop{\overline{z}}}\,\And {{a}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}=|z{{|}^{2}}$

\(\therefore \frac{1}{z}=\frac{\bar{z}}{|z|^2}\)

concepts.title: Procedure to calculate square root of a complex number
concepts.conceptBody:
Square roots of a complex number 

Let $z=a+ib$ be the complex number of which we want to find out the root, then

$\sqrt{a+ib}=x+iy$

Squaring

$a+ib={{(x+iy)}^{2}}=\left( {{x}^{2}}-{{y}^{2}} \right)+2ixy$

equating real & imaginary coefficients on both sides

$a=x^2-y^2$ …(i)

$b=2xy$ …(ii)

now ${{x}^{2}}+{{y}^{2}}=\sqrt{{{\left( {{x}^{2}}-{{y}^{2}} \right)}^{2}}+4{{x}^{2}}{{y}^{2}}}$

$=\sqrt{{{\text{a}}^{2}}+{{\text{b}}^{2}}}$ …(iii)

from (i), (ii) & (iii)

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {{x}^{2}}=\frac{1}{2}\left\{ a+\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}} \right\}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\And \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{y}^{2}}=\frac{1}{2}\left\{ \sqrt{{{a}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}}-a \right\} \\ \Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }x\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }=\pm \sqrt{\left( \frac{1}{2} \right)\left\{ a+\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}} \right\}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }y=\pm \sqrt{\frac{1}{2}\left\{ \sqrt{{{a}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}}-a \right\}} \\ \therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\sqrt{a+ib}=\pm \left\{ \sqrt{\frac{1}{2}\left( \sqrt{{{a}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}}+a \right)}+i\sqrt{\left( \frac{1}{2} \right)\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}}-a} \right\} \\ \end{array}$

if $b>0$, because if $b>0$ then $xy>0$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ x}\And \text{y}$ are both + ve or - ve.

$= \pm\left\{\sqrt{\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) \sqrt{a^2+b^2}+a}-i \sqrt{\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) \sqrt{a^2+b^2}-a}\right\}$

if $b<0, x y<0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad x>0, y<0$

or $\quad \mathrm{x}<0, \mathrm{y}>0$

We can also write.

\(\sqrt{a+i b}= \pm\left\{\sqrt{\frac{1}{2}(|z|)+a} \pm i \sqrt{\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)(|z|-a)}\right\}\)
